etymology.description: 酉(酉) (yǒu, "bottle") + 人(人) (rén, "person") + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") = a person drinking from a bottle -> mouth separated, becoming 今(今) + body becoming 欠(欠) -> phonetic 酓(酓) (yǐn) + semantic 欠(欠, "yawn") -> clerical script switched 酓 with 食(食, "eat")
etymology.interpretation: The original character depicted a person drinking from a bottle, with droplets representing the liquid involved. This visualization evolved as components separated and transformed, with the character eventually being reinterpreted as a phono-semantic compound combining elements that convey the act of drinking.
etymology.simplification: 饮 is simplified from 飲 by replacing 食(食) (shí, "eat") with the radical 饣 (a simplified form of the food radical 食) and retaining the phonetic component 欠(欠) (qiàn, "yawn").

examples.chinese: 他每天喝大量的水(他每天喝大量的水)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān hē dàliàng de shuǐ.
examples.english: He drinks a large amount of water every day.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢喝绿茶(她喜歡喝綠茶)，尤其是在早上(尤其是在早上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān hē lǜchá, yóuqí shì zài zǎoshang.
examples.english: She likes to drink green tea, especially in the morning.

examples.chinese: 在这个宴会上(在這個宴會上)，我们都饮了很多酒(我們都飲了很多酒)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhège yànhuì shàng, wǒmen dōu yǐnle hěn duō jiǔ.
examples.english: At this banquet, we all drank a lot of wine.
